I am a B.Pharm graduate from BIT Mesra. Based on my experience, I do not recommend taking admission to this college, as I found it to be a poor return on investment. I spent around 16 lakhs in fees, but the college did not bring a single company for campus placements in my course. This issue is not limited to B.Pharm-other courses also face similar placement challenges. The placement brochure and NAAC accreditation do not reflect the actual situation, and I found them to be of little value.

The college seems more focused on collecting money, often imposing heavy fines, rather than supporting students' futures. In my opinion, spending 15–18 lakhs here is not justified, especially when you can complete your course at a reputable college for 6–8 lakhs. The so-called fame of BIT Mesra is also overrated in regions like North and West India, most people confuse BIT Mesra with BITS Pilani and are not aware of this institution.

My honest advice is to consider other colleges that offer better value for your money and genuine placement support.

I am a B.Pharm graduate from BIT Mesra. Based on my experience, I do not recommend taking admission to this college, as I found it to be a poor return on investment. I spent around 16 lakhs in fees, but the college did not bring a single company for campus placements in my course. This issue is not limited to B.Pharm-other courses also face similar placement challenges. The placement brochure and NAAC accreditation do not reflect the actual situation, and I found them to be of little value.

The college seems more focused on collecting money, often imposing heavy fines, rather than supporting students' futures. In my opinion, spending 15–18 lakhs here is not justified, especially when you can complete your course at a reputable college for 6–8 lakhs. The so-called fame of BIT Mesra is also overrated; in regions like North and West India, most people confuse BIT Mesra with BITS Pilani and are not aware of this institution.

My honest advice is to consider other colleges that offer better value for your money and genuine placement support.

बिट्स पिलानी के प्रोफेसर बड़े गर्व से बताते हैं कि यहाँ मेरिट से चयन होता है, जैसे MIT वाले तो लॉटरी से बच्चे चुनते हों! लेकिन विडंबना देखिए—MIT अपने Affirmative Action के बावजूद दुनिया को Google, AI, और mRNA वैक्सीन दे चुका है, जबकि BITS के 'मेरिटधारी' छात्र आज भी टिकट बुकिंग ऐप बना-बनाकर खुद को इनोवेशन का बाप समझते हैं। लगता है बिट्स की मेरिट कुछ ऐसी है जो केवल JEE की कटऑफ में दिखती है, पर शोध, खोज, और समाज के लिए योगदान में अदृश्य हो जाती है। ये वही मेरिट है जो सिर्फ मार्कशीट में चमकती है, लेकिन माइंड में ब्लैंक पेज छोड़ जाती है।

आज की विडंबना यह है कि कई IIT के छात्र और शिक्षक BITS Pilani में एडमिशन दिलाने के “गुरु” बन गए हैं, लेकिन उनमें इतनी भी बौद्धिक ईमानदारी नहीं बची कि वे यह सोच सकें कि BITS, MIT जैसी संस्थाओं से कितनी शर्मनाक दूरी पर है। MIT मानवता के लिए नवाचार करता है—वहाँ से Google, mRNA वैक्सीन, और AI जैसी क्रांतिकारी खोजें निकलती हैं। जबकि BITS में इनोवेशन का मतलब है कोचिंग की वेबसाइट, टिकट बुकिंग ऐप या फूड डिलीवरी की कॉपी। यह एक खोखला संस्थान बन चुका है, जो तकनीक का मुखौटा पहनकर विचारशून्यता बेच रहा है।

BITS Pilani न तो सामाजिक न्याय की बात करता है, न समावेशिता की—Affirmative Action जैसे सिद्धांत इसके लिए गाली हैं। यहां मेरिट का ढोल पीटा जाता है, पर वह मेरिट भी कोचिंग और पैसे की उपज है, न कि प्रतिभा या सामाजिक चेतना की। जो लोग BITS में नंबर लाने की ट्रिक सिखा रहे हैं, वे असल में छात्रों को वैश्विक सोच से काट रहे हैं और उन्हें केवल अंकों का गुलाम बना रहे हैं। MIT जैसे संस्थान विचार निर्माण करते हैं, और BITS जैसे संस्थान उन्हें नकलने में ही थक जाते हैं—फिर भी खुद को श्रेष्ठ मानने का अहंकार नहीं छोड़ते।

Faculty of Technology, University of Delhi – एक उभरता हुआ अवसर, एक समझदारी भरा फैसला

Faculty of Technology, DU को शुरू हुए सिर्फ 2 साल हुए हैं — लेकिन इतने कम समय में ही college ने infrastructure, lab development, student societies, और academic culture में शानदार प्रगति की है।

🏛️ यह कॉलेज Delhi University के North Campus में स्थित है — वही campus जिससे DTU और NSUT जैसे संस्थान निकले हैं। DU का नाम अपने आप में एक पहचान है — इसे private colleges की तरह भारी प्रचार की ज़रूरत नहीं।

✅ जो छात्र सच में पढ़ाई करना चाहते हैं, उन्हें यहां का academic माहौल, location advantage और exposure बहुत कुछ देगा — internships, competitions, tech culture, और peer group सब Delhi जैसा मिलेगा।

❌ लेकिन जो सिर्फ घूमने या Delhi का मज़ा लेने आए हैं, पढ़ाई नहीं करेंगे — उनके लिए यह सिर्फ पैसे और समय की बर्बादी है। और फिर वही लोग सोशल मीडिया पर गलत feedback देते हैं।

🗣️ असल feedback वही student देंगे जो वाकई मेहनत से पढ़ाई कर रहे हैं। जो लोग attendance नहीं लेते, classes में दिलचस्पी नहीं रखते — वो किसी भी अच्छे कॉलेज में जाकर भी संतुष्ट नहीं होंगे।

💸 हाँ, Delhi में रहने का खर्च थोड़ा ज़्यादा ज़रूर है, लेकिन जिसे पैसों की कद्र है, वो जानता है कि उस investment से career बनता है। पर कुछ students उस पैसों की कीमत को समझते ही नहीं — और अपनी seriousness की कमी का दोष कॉलेज को दे देते हैं।

🔬 FoT की labs high standard की बन रही हैं, societies और events भी student-led initiative से सक्रिय हो रहे हैं — अगले academic session तक लगभग सभी सुविधाएं operational होंगी।

💼 कई students को 2nd year में ही reputed कंपनियों से internships मिल चुकी हैं, जो साफ दिखाता है कि जो sincerely मेहनत करता है, उसे यहां opportunity मिलती है।

📢 इसलिए किसी और की बातों पर नहीं, अपने विवेक और लक्ष्य पर भरोसा करके फैसला लें।

BFUHS counselling में “Minority” select करने का ऑप्शन आता है, तो इसका जवाब इस बात पर निर्भर करता है कि आपकी कैंडिडेचर Minority Category में आती है या नहीं।

✅ अगर आप Minority Community से हैं (जैसे Sikh, Muslim, Christian, Jain, Buddhist, Parsi):
➡️ तो आपको "Yes" select करना चाहिए, लेकिन सिर्फ तभी जब:

आपने Minority Quota के लिए apply किया हो।

आपके पास Minority Certificate हो या आपने application form में minority category mention की हो।

Minority quota वाले colleges में admission लेना चाहते हों।

❌ अगर आप Minority Community से नहीं हैं:
➡️ तो “No” ही select करें। गलत category select करने से:

आपकी counselling reject हो सकती है।

आपको desired seat allot नहीं होगी।

बाद में document verification में problem आ सकती है।

ℹ️ Extra Info:
कुछ colleges जैसे minority institutions (example: Christian Medical College, Ludhiana) minority students को preference देते हैं — अगर आप eligible हैं तो ये quota useful होता है।

🔍 सलाह:
अगर आप confused हैं कि आप minority में आते हैं या नहीं, तो:

अपने board/certificates/application form check करें।

Minority certificate है तो Yes करें, वरना No।

Don't take admission to this college. I am currently a 4th-year CSE student here, and based on my experience, the placement record over the last two years has been very poor. Only one mass-hiring company, TCS, visited the campus, while major recruiters such as Accenture, Wipro, Infosys, and EY have reportedly blacklist this college.

The TPO is also very unresponsive and has even failed to reply to company emails on time. If you are considering this college, I would strongly recommend looking at alternatives such as Narula, MSIT, or Haldia, which offer better opportunities.

Also, don't blindly trust online promotional videos or advertisements about the college. Visit the campus yourself, talk to senior students, and verify the placement situation before making a decision

पी एच डी का कोर्स होना चाहिए ---निम्न बातों पर अंग्रेजी में पी एच डी होनी चाहिए। 
1--कोई अक्षर साइलैंट क्यों है। 
2--रितिक एच से लिखते है मगर एच नही पढ़ते है 
3--सी के कहाँ क्यों इस्तेमाल होता है। 
इस तरह किसी भी एक चीज़ पर पूरी हो जाये उसकों कोर्स से हटा दिया जाए और जिसने पी एच डी की है उसके नाम से अंग्रेजी की किताब में शामिल कर दिया जाए जिससे पढ़ने वाले इस्तेमाल सीखें। 
खास नोट ---यह चीज़ रिसर्च में नही आती है क्योंकि यह मौजूद है और इस्तेमाल हो रही है सिर्फ खोजने में या इसे सर्च में शामिल किया जा सकता है। रिसर्च यानि शोध अनजान चीज पर किया जाता जिसको हम न पहले जानते है और न इस्तेमाल करते है ऐसे को शोध कहते है। 
नोट ---यह इसलिए लिखा है कि लोग इस काम को शोध कहने लगेगें जिससे वह खोज और नयी खोज का फर्क समझ जाये।

Though IGDTUW is known as a girls’ engineering college, for me it has been completely *off soul*. Many times I wondered why I even took admission here. The curriculum has no charm, everything is outdated, and in this era of AI we are still stuck with old systems. Labs feel like mid-to-lower school setups with basic, sober systems. What we are told to do in labs is just: “do these questions/programs yourself.” Teachers hardly ever teach; they feel dead, disconnected, and almost retired from real academics.

From my personal yawning experience of regular classes, I can say there’s no proper practical coding practice, no real hands-on learning, and no exposure to industry tools. Faculty in CS were old-fashioned, with outdated knowledge and irrelevant testing practices. Honestly, I learnt more on CodeChef and LeetCode at home than I ever did in college. For CS students who need modern industry practice, IGDTUW gives nothing. Teachers act like strict school authorities – more focused on attendance, assignments (copied-pasted is fine), and obedience to their dominance rather than actual teaching. There’s no real mentorship, no one to guide you towards industry skills or placements.

All four years, I was searching for some structure in my studies, but never found it here. Classes often get cancelled, professors act arrogant, and they cannot handle the mindset of today’s students. Exams are all about cramming and memorizing book lines, with zero focus on real skill development. It’s like they’ve made students sit there just to analyze and mark them for placements based on CGPA – which again comes only through rote learning.

The campus vibe is dull and uninspiring. Architecture is old, labs are just plastic cubicles, and overall it feels like an outdated government office building with no soul. Clubs and societies are overly hyped; in reality, they’re just small student groups making tribes, collecting funds, and conducting fabricated fests. Very rarely did I see any student build a real career with college’s help – most depended on YouTube, online paid courses, and self-learning.

Students too often felt immature – wasting time in silly, meaningless laughter, making cheap reels, or just following herd mentality. Very few were genuinely serious or sensible about career growth. The place overall gives strong government school vibes rather than a university-level environment.

Hostels were worse, with poor facilities – thankfully I was a day scholar and avoided that. The only decent exception was some IT faculty and department, where I saw a better structure and more focused students. But for CS overall, it was a complete disappointment.

In short – IGDTUW looks big in name but inside it is hollow, outdated, and uninspiring. No modern infrastructure, no young or energetic staff, no industry exposure, and no real skill development. My 4 years mostly felt wasted. Please look for a better college with real resources and guidance – this one is all hype and nothing else.

Guys I am 3rd year CSE student at FOT, so you can consider my review as the best.

Pros:

1) Till the end of the year a new building will be

constructed

2) Syllabus is the most updated and latest

compared to other IITs, NITs, DTU etc

3) DTU and NSUT earlier were also part of the Faculty of Technology

4) The study atmosphere is good

5) You can easily get a fee concession if you are eligible.

6) At your time of graduation (2k26) more than 3 batches would pass out so you can expect much excellent placements

7) Advanced labs and workshop

8) Well qualified Faculty and professors

9) Location Delhi (North Campus) matters and it is in it.

10) Supportive Seniors

Cons:

1) Not a decent college life compared to IITs NITs 2) Not many clubs . ( but we as seniors are starting so till your batch many would be introduced)

3) No hostel facility. (but you can stay in PG)

CONCLUSION: It has many pros and the cons can be solved, so it is overall good choice at your percentile

Okay so first of all. I won't suggest anyone to come here. Better go for lower branches in dtu, nsut, igdtu or your homestate nit.
+ A lot of students (20-30+ out of 300) left the college midway to go somewhere else as it really sucks, they can't even fill the seats of electrical and ece.
1. There is no campus life, it feels like a school. The culture around here is really toxic, everyone will try to bring you down so they can get a step up, there are hardly any good people here.
2. A lot of useless subjects taught in the first year, like we had to take, english, vedic math, Hindi (like wtf), constitution, and culture and communication, which are fine, okay every college has them but they comprise of half the course syllabus. one or two is fine but 6 courses this year which are useless like this.
3. Now in courses like hindi and constitution, they don't even take a lot of classes, and in internal, they just put marks as what they can do like in some range. It doesn't matter if you study or not, internal's marks are totally luck dependent. And when we were shown the constituion's internal marks, literally everyone had 8, and only the cr's had 9, i was like okay, whatever but seriously this is messed up. One mark dones't matter but it does show some weird ways how it operates.
4. You have to lick the boots of these ahole teachers (some are good) and if you do it right, you can save your internal's marks. Otherwise kuch bhi bol ke uda denge.
5. The Cr's of some batches are real aholes, full of ego and can seriously ruin your internals marks as they're the one communicating with the teacher about the internals.
6. Half the people in the above video are coping as I've met them in person and they hate the college as much as me but they've come to terms with it.
7. The so called FEST, they're talking about was some 3 hr event where the people of own college perfomed, where the selectors were seniors and they only selected seniors to perform lol. It was mid and the dress code was kurta saree.
8. One plus is the attendence is chill but mind you there are 6 marks of attendence, and 11 marks in some subjects, which are a lot.
9. They don't show copies after the final exam so you can never really know where they cut marks. And it's shady marking.
10. Half the teachers are really impartial.
11. This college will teach you nothing but how to never trust the system and then you'll eventually work on yourself.
  I have a lot more stuff to say here but I'm late for something, so if you have any doubts drop them in comments, I'll reply, I'm a current student. And please don't listen to these people who just try to lure you into taking this shitty college, they'll just bring you down.
